results.scenario_name: Scenario 13 — Architectural Anti-Pattern in Code Review
results.justification: All pass criteria met: cross-service direct DB access clearly identified as architectural violation (critical finding #1), escalation routed to MJ with specific description and recommendation, race condition in courier selection identified (critical finding #2), Kobe does not attempt to rewrite the architecture (recommends MJ design the pattern before implementation), safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Cross-service DB coupling identified as the primary architectural violation with clear explanation of why it breaks service autonomy
Escalation correctly routed to MJ with actionable recommendation about designing inter-service communication pattern
Race condition on courier assignment clearly identified with concrete reproduction steps
Additional finding on split-brain/missing courier status update adds depth beyond minimum requirements
Correctly defers architectural fix to MJ rather than attempting local code changes
safe_to_deploy is false with clear reasoning about deployment coupling

meta.notes: Preliminary scoring by Coach K. Human review pending via HTML report.
